hero.title: Examples
hero.subtitle: Real-world patterns you'll actually use in production — in pure HTML.

login.title: Login with JWT
login.desc: A complete login flow: form validation, POST to auth endpoint, save the JWT to a global store, and automatically attach the token to every subsequent request via a request interceptor.
login.previewHint: Fill both fields to try the interactive preview

dashboard.title: Protected Route & Token Validation
dashboard.desc: A route guarded by the auth store, paired with a response interceptor that acts as a control script: on every API call, if the server returns 401 or 403, the token is invalidated and the user is redirected to login automatically — no extra code needed in the route itself.
dashboard.interceptorNote: If any request returns 401 or 403, the interceptor clears the token and redirects to /login automatically.

search.title: Live Search
search.desc: An instant search input that fires a debounced GET request on every keystroke, rendering results reactively. No addEventListener, no setTimeout, no DOM manipulation.

cart.title: Shopping Cart
cart.desc: A global store shared between a product list and a cart badge — in different parts of the page. When a product is added, the badge and the cart summary update simultaneously, with no event bus or shared component needed.
cart.addHint: Add a product to see the cart

polling.title: Live Polling
polling.desc: A server-status dashboard that refreshes automatically every 5 seconds using the refresh attribute. Conditional styling reacts instantly to the current health state — no setInterval, no fetch loop.

cta.title: Want More Examples?
cta.subtitle: Check out the full documentation for advanced patterns.
